query: Can a 62-year-old retire at 65 on a 1.4M portfolio plus social security?

questions.question: Planning horizon in years?
questions.why: Drawdown variance compounds non-linearly with horizon length.

questions.question: What annual real spending in today's dollars?
questions.why: Spending is the largest single driver of failure probability.

questions.question: Any major one-time costs expected (home repair, healthcare event)?
questions.why: Episodic shocks need probabilistic event gates, not mean smoothing.

questions.question: How is the portfolio allocated (stocks / bonds / cash)?
questions.why: Asset allocation drives the central return assumption and the variance.
